pull.title: Remove self refs in softmax for-loop
pull.body:
Attempted running flash attn cute on H100s with the following package versions:

```
nvidia-cutlass-dsl==4.2.0
flash_attn @ flash_attn-2.8.3%2Bcu12torch2.8cxx11abiFALSE-cp312-cp312-linux_x86_64.whl #sha256=2605b2653e7b9d6615bbb154896ae4d3f0b95d3120af7a6374ee97092e61df02
```

It appears the CuTE jit compiler isn't able to handle `self` in the `online_softmax` for-loop. Not very familiar with CuTE, but it seems like refactoring all references to `self` outside the for-loop in softmax does the trick. Open to suggestions and better ways of fixing this. 

This doesn't seem to be an issue on B200s. Changing `nvidia-cutlass-dsl` version didn't help either.



```
.../site-packages/flash_attn/cute/interface.py:563: in flash_attn_varlen_func
    return FlashAttnVarlenFunc.apply(
.../site-packages/torch/autograd/function.py:575: in apply
    return super().apply(*args, **kwargs)  # type: ignore[misc]
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
.../site-packages/flash_attn/cute/interface.py:491: in forward
    out, lse = _flash_attn_fwd(

.../site-packages/nvidia_cutlass_dsl/python_packages/cutlass/base_dsl/compiler.py:288: in compile

           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

    return scf_gen.scf_execute_dynamic(
.../site-packages/nvidia_cutlass_dsl/python_packages/cutlass/cutlass_dsl/cutlass_ast_decorators.py:211: in scf_execute_dynamic
    region_result = builder(

                    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
.../site-packages/nvidia_cutlass_dsl/python_packages/cutlass/base_dsl/dsl.py:265: in __call__

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _

mixed_values = [<cutlass.cute.core._Tensor object at 0x7e7280e42b10>, <flash_attn.cute.softmax.Softmax object at 0x7e7288381d50>], body_name = 'for', full_write_args_count = 2

    def unpack_to_irvalue(
        mixed_values: List[Any], body_name: str, full_write_args_count: int
    ) -> Tuple[List[ir.Value], PyTreeDef]:
        log().debug("===--- Values UNPack")
        for idx, packed in enumerate(mixed_values):
            log().debug("[%d]: will-unpacked: [type:%s] %s", idx, type(packed), packed)

        try:
            unpacked_values, treedef = tree_flatten(
                remove_read_only_frozen_dataclass(mixed_values, full_write_args_count)
            )
        except DSLTreeFlattenError as e:
>           raise DSLRuntimeError(
                f"The '{body_name}' statement encountered a user-defined Python object, which cannot be automatically converted into an dynamic expression.",
                context={
                    e.message: (
                        f"All expressions within '{body_name}' must be dynamic expressions, "
                        "mixing Python objects and dynamic expressions is not supported. "
                        "The DSL failed to convert the Python object into dynamic expressions."
                    )
                },
                suggestion=(
                    f"Please ensure '{e.type_str}' implements the '{DynamicExpression.__name__}' or mark with `dataclass`, "
                    f"so it can be treated as a valid dynamic expression or mark '{body_name}' as a constant expression if conditions are Python objects."
                ),
            )
E           cutlass.base_dsl.common.DSLRuntimeError: DSLRuntimeError: The 'for' statement encountered a user-defined Python object, which cannot be automatically converted into an dynamic expression.
E           🔍 Additional Context:
E
E               Flatten Error: All expressions within 'for' must be dynamic expressions, mixing Python objects and dynamic expressions is not supported. The DSL failed to convert the Python object into dynamic expressions.
E           💡 Suggestions:
E            Please ensure 'flash_attn.cute.softmax.Softmax' implements the 'DynamicExpression' or mark with `dataclass`, so it can be treated as a valid dynamic expression or mark 'for' as a constant expression if conditions are Python objects.

.../site-packages/nvidia_cutlass_dsl/python_packages/cutlass/cutlass_dsl/cutlass.py:743: DSLRuntimeError
```
